reasoning: 4-year-old female presenting with fever and new rash since yesterday. Parent reports lethargy and refusal to eat. The combination of fever plus rash in a young child requires urgent evaluation to rule out serious bacterial infections including meningococcal disease and sepsis. Rash onset within 24 hours of fever onset is a red-flag pattern. Age under 5 increases vulnerability to rapid deterioration.
risk_amplifiers_detected: Fever with rash in child under 5 — red-flag combination
Lethargy and reduced oral intake — possible early decompensation
Rapid symptom progression (rash appeared day after fever onset)
